campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council debated the proposal before voting on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance Department presented the annual tax rate update
Council will vote on whether to approve the new rates
Changes take effect in the next fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council decided to move forward by skipping the usual first step of the approval process and scheduling the final vote on the new ordinance for the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input on a proposed ordinance
Council waived the first reading requirement to speed up the approval process
New ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at the next council meeting
Ordinance added to consent calendar (fast-track items)

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment reports
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors (QLM, Inc. and others)
Approved agreements for professional services (three-year contract mentioned)
Directed preparation of town improvement projects

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 28, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on April 28, 2026, to approve several items including allowing free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for a community luncheon event. The council also reviewed agreements affecting city employees, including a deal with park rangers and security initiatives, plus discussed rules about affordable housing when city programs end.
keyTopics: Free use of Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for Together San José community luncheon
New contract terms with park rangers (POPRA)
Bay Area security initiative agreement
Affordable housing protections when city programs expire

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to review a draft plan for improving biodiversity and urban forests in the city. They also recognized the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center for winning a state award and proclaimed April as Fair Housing Month.
keyTopics: Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan - draft being reviewed for final approval
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center receives California Parks and Recreation Society Award of Excellence
Fair Housing Month proclamation - city commitment to housing discrimination prevention

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate and comment both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
Online and in-person participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 27, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each. The council also approved meeting minutes from January and reviewed how the city's pension and retiree health benefit investments performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period open to residents on any city matter
Approval of January 26 Audit Committee meeting minutes
Pension and retiree health benefit investment performance report (Q1 2026)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be put on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new employment agreement with the city's firefighters through 2029. One item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract agreement covering 2026-2029
Staff withdrew one agenda item before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to discuss several important financial and service matters. The council reviewed tax rules, approved a budget for the next fiscal year, updated user fees, and received a presentation about efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
User and service fee updates
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Meeting minutes and upcoming calendar review

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to take another look at a tree removal permit that was already approved for a property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to reconsider the decision that allowed trees on this lot to be cut down.
keyTopics: Tree removal permit reconsideration at 751 Waverley Street
Protected tree protection and oversight
Director's review process for approved permits
